sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'c', 'W', 'G']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")

# Check ascii to see what's above
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(24, 33):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][14:28] if len(ascii_lines[i]) > 28 else ascii_lines[i]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- id=1: c at (24,20)-(27,23) - the previously collected center square (now charcoal again with blue center)
- id=5: G at (28,16)-(31,19) - left blue square (dark gray frame)
- id=6: W at (28,20)-(31,20) - white strip blocking the way
- id=7: N at (28,21)-(31,23) - player
The white strip (id=6) is at col 20, rows 28-31. The player is at cols 21-23, rows 28-31.
To get to the left blue square, I need to go around the white strip. I can:
1. Go UP (above row 28), then LEFT, then DOWN
2. Go DOWN (below row 31), then LEFT, then UP
Looking at the ascii, row 24-27 has the collected center square at cols 20-23. So going UP would put me next to that square.
Row 32 is empty. So going DOWN would be easier.
Let me try: DOWN, LEFT, LEFT, UP to get to the left of the left blue square, then... hmm, actually I want to be adjacent to the left blue square to collect it.
The left blue square is at (28,16)-(31,19). To be adjacent from the left, I'd need to be at col 15. To be adjacent from above, I'd need to be at row 27. To be adjacent from below, I'd need to be at row 32.
Let me try going DOWN, then LEFT twice to get to col 16-19 area, then UP to be below the left blue square, then UP again to be adjacent from below.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The first time I collected the center square, the player was BELOW it. The player was at rows 41-43 and the square was at rows 36-39. So the player was 2 rows below the square.
For the left blue square at rows 28-31, I need to be at rows 32-35 (below it). Currently the player is at rows 28-31, same level as the square.
So I need to:
1. Move DOWN to get below the square
2. Move LEFT to align columns
3. Press SPACE to collect
But the white strip is blocking LEFT movement. Let me go DOWN first, then LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Go DOWN first to get below the white strip, then LEFT to align with left blue square
action(['DOWN', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
